title: stop: Do nothing if container was never created in runtime or in a invalid state.
body:
Following commit ensures we silently return container id on `stop` if
container was never created in OCI runtime.

Following behavior ensures that we are in parity with docker.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11740

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Please do not do this in Libpod, but rather in the CLI (or perhaps in
pkg/infra?) - we return a sensible error already and that seems correct to
me. The frontend should ignore this error and exit cleanly.

@mheon All  handling for other states is also done in the same function so just adding it here to keep things consistent.

@mheon moved it to frontend where we are ignoring other errors.

PS: this is already handled for `tunnel` hence only making changes in `abi`.

The Libpod APIs always return an error when something goes wrong. Not
returning an error when no action is taken is inconsistent with the rest of
the API.

This particular bug is a returning one. I remember fixing it in the 1.x
days with a fix identical to the one I describe. Evidently it didn’t
survive the 2.0 rewrites.
